mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of months with falling rates.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle alleviates supply chain bottlenecks and stabilizes input costs, allowing corporate margins to expand and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, a concurrent rise in the unemployment rate triggers recession risk pricing, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in more than 70% of weeks where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and UNRATE increases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls growth falling below 100,000 signals labor market deceleration and reduced wage pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of months where PAYEMS growth is below 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 250, a concurrent increase in the federal funds rate signals aggressive inflation fighting that suppresses growth expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of quarters where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 and FEDFUNDS rises.
